title: EMPLOYMENT RELATED DAYCARE - 211info
text: LAKEVIEW CHILD WELFARE AND SELF-SUFFICIENCY EMPLOYMENT RELATED DAYCARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Employment Related Day Care (ERDC) provides a subsidy to working families to cover the cost of child care to provide access to quality child care to those working and caring for a child. Assistance is available when funds are available. 108 E Street N Lakeview, OR 97630 Eligibility: This program is for families that: -Live in Oregon. -Are working or in school or receiving TANF -In a two-parent family, both parents must be working or in school. (This does not apply to parents receiving TANF.) -There are some exceptions for two parent households where one parent cannot provide child care because of a medical or mental health condition, or if supervised contact is required by the State. -Need child care to keep working, attending school, or participating in family well-being activities. Have a child who is: -Under 13 years old and needs child care, or -13 to 17 years old and needs care because of special circumstances. -Meet income limits. -Initial income limits 200% FPL. Ongoing and Exit limit 250% FPL. See website for income chart. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm, closed major holidays Email (541) 947-3376 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 947-3376 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Complete an application and have an interview with a DHS worker -Print out, complete, and mail the application by using the website -Walk into an ODHS Self-Sufficiency Office (Processing Centers do not offer walk-in services) -Call your local ODHS Self-Sufficiency Office and ask for an application to be mailed to you FEES: ERDC is a subsidy program. This means many families still pay part of the child care cost. This is called a copayment (copay). The copay amount depends on family size and income. DOCUMENTS: The documents required for verification will vary by family situation LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement. Application form is available in English, English Large Print, Spanish, Spanish Large Print, Arabic, Burmese, Nepali, Russian, Somali, Vietnamese. AREA SERVED: Klamath and Lake Counties SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Services: Child Care Expense Assistance Child Care Expense Assistance * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Other Services Offered At This Location: INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CHILD ABUSE REPORTING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CHILD WELFARE SERVICES MUTUAL SUPPORT - FOSTER, ADOPT, AND KINSHIP PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - SNAP PROGRAM PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - SUMMER EBT PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - TEMPORARY ASSISTANCE FOR NEEDY FAMILIES PUBLIC ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS - TEMPORARY ASSISTANCE TO DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SURVIVORS If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. LAKEVIEW CHILD WELFARE AND SELF-SUFFICIENCY
